title: SACRAMENTO, CA

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1138 raises the base tax credit rate from 20% to 35% for film productions and from 25% to 40% for productions relocating from other jurisdictions.",
    "The law increases the overall qualified expenditures for films, TV shows, and mini-series applying to the California Film and Television Tax Credit Program.",
    "AB 1138 maintains tax uplifts in current programs, including a 5% uplift for filming outside the Los Angeles Zone.",
    "The legislation provides up to a 2% uplift to productions that hire 1 to 4 trainees without displacing anticipated hiring of experienced workers.",
    "AB 1138 expands the definition of 'Qualified Motion Picture' to include 20-minute television shows, animation, reboots, and certain large-scale competition shows.",
    "The bill eliminates the 50% ownership or 10-year lease requirement for productions utilizing a certified sound stage in the Soundstage Film Tax Credit Program.",
    "AB 1138 increases the set aside for independent productions from 8% under the current program to 10%.",
    "The California Film Commission will receive more flexibility to move tax credit dollars across different categories to address current demands.",
    "The existing program's reporting requirements will be strengthened to ensure that an employee's voluntarily given veteran status and the aggregated ZIP codes of employees on a production are reported to the Film Commission.",
    "Governor Newsom's proposed budget expansion includes an increase of the program from $330 million to $750 million."
  ]
}
```
